journal_entry.reason: Cash borrowed from bank creates a loan liability

output.liability_increase_reason: Notes Payable (or Bank Loan Payable) increases by $200,000 when the company borrows cash from the bank
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity transaction occurs; this is a borrowing transaction, not an owner investment or retained earnings increase
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is earned; borrowing cash creates a liability obligation, not income from business operations
output.asset_decrease_reason: No asset decreases on the credit side; cash is received (debit side), and no other assets are given up
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividend or owner withdrawal transaction is reversed or reduced
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expense is reversed or reduced; the interest expense will be recognized later when incurred over time, not at the borrowing date

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash increases by $200,000 from the bank loan proceeds received.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividend distributions in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses are incurred at the time of borrowing. Interest expense will be recognized later when it accrues, not at loan inception.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or extinguished. This transaction creates a new liability rather than decreasing one.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are affected by this borrowing transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are affected by this borrowing transaction.

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Transaction is a bank loan borrowing. No tax is mentioned in the text. Loans and financing transactions are not taxable supplies under tax law.
